contents.value:
```kotlin
fun println(message: Any?): Unit
```
Prints the given message and a newline to standard output.

```kotlin
fun print(message: Any?): Unit
```
Prints the given message to standard output without a newline.

```kotlin
fun readLine(): String?
```
Reads a line from standard input, returns null if EOF reached.

```kotlin
fun <T> listOf(vararg elements: T): List<T>
```
Returns a new read-only list of given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> mutableListOf(vararg elements: T): MutableList<T>
```
Returns a new MutableList with the given elements.

```kotlin
fun <K, V> mapOf(vararg pairs: Pair<K, V>): Map<K, V>
```
Returns a new read-only map with the specified key-value pairs.

```kotlin
fun <K, V> mutableMapOf(vararg pairs: Pair<K, V>): MutableMap<K, V>
```
Returns a new MutableMap with the specified key-value pairs.

```kotlin
fun <T> setOf(vararg elements: T): Set<T>
```
Returns a new read-only set with the given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> mutableSetOf(vararg elements: T): MutableSet<T>
```
Returns a new MutableSet with the given elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> arrayOf(vararg elements: T): Array<T>
```
Returns an array containing the specified elements.

```kotlin
fun intArrayOf(vararg elements: Int): IntArray
```
Returns an IntArray containing the specified elements.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> T.let(block: (T) -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` value as its argument and returns its result. Often used for null checks and transformations.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> T.run(block: T.() -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` value as its receiver and returns its result.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> with(receiver: T, block: T.() -> R): R
```
Calls the specified function block with the given receiver as `this` and returns its result.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.apply(block: T.() -> Unit): T
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` as its receiver and returns `this`. Used for object configuration.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.also(block: (T) -> Unit): T
```
Calls the specified function block with `this` as its argument and returns `this`. Used for performing side effects.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.takeIf(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): T?
```
Returns `this` if it satisfies the given predicate or null if it doesn't.

```kotlin
fun <T> T.takeUnless(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): T?
```
Returns `this` if it does NOT satisfy the given predicate or null if it does.

```kotlin
fun repeat(times: Int, action: (Int) -> Unit)
```
Executes the given function action the specified number of times.

```kotlin
fun require(value: Boolean, lazyMessage: () -> Any): Unit
```
Throws an IllegalArgumentException if the value is false.

```kotlin
fun check(value: Boolean, lazyMessage: () -> Any): Unit
```
Throws an IllegalStateException if the value is false.

```kotlin
fun error(message: Any): Nothing
```
Throws an IllegalStateException with the given message.

```kotlin
fun TODO(reason: String): Nothing
```
Always throws NotImplementedError stating that operation is not implemented.

```kotlin
fun <T> lazy(initializer: () -> T): Lazy<T>
```
Creates a new Lazy instance that uses the specified initialization function. Value computed on first access.

```kotlin
fun <T> sequence(block: suspend SequenceScope<T>.() -> Unit): Sequence<T>
```
Builds a Sequence lazily yielding values one by one.

```kotlin
fun <E> buildList(builderAction: MutableList<E>.() -> Unit): List<E>
```
Builds a new read-only List by populating a MutableList using the given builder action.

```kotlin
fun <K, V> buildMap(builderAction: MutableMap<K, V>.() -> Unit): Map<K, V>
```
Builds a new read-only Map by populating a MutableMap using the given builder action.

```kotlin
fun <E> buildSet(builderAction: MutableSet<E>.() -> Unit): Set<E>
```
Builds a new read-only Set by populating a MutableSet using the given builder action.

```kotlin
fun <T> emptyList(): List<T>
```
Returns an empty read-only list.

```kotlin
fun <K, V> emptyMap(): Map<K, V>
```
Returns an empty read-only map.

```kotlin
fun <T> emptySet(): Set<T>
```
Returns an empty read-only set.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.forEach(action: (T) -> Unit)
```
Performs the given action on each element.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.map(transform: (T) -> R): List<R>
```
Returns a list containing the results of applying the given transform function to each element.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.filter(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): List<T>
```
Returns a list containing only elements matching the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.flatMap(transform: (T) -> Iterable<R>): List<R>
```
Returns a single list of all elements yielded from results of transform function on each element.

```kotlin
fun <S, T : S> Iterable<T>.reduce(operation: (acc: S, T) -> S): S
```
Accumulates value starting with the first element and applying operation from left to right.

```kotlin
fun <T, R> Iterable<T>.fold(initial: R, operation: (acc: R, T) -> R): R
```
Accumulates value starting with initial value and applying operation from left to right.

```kotlin
fun <T, K> Iterable<T>.groupBy(keySelector: (T) -> K): Map<K, List<T>>
```
Groups elements of the collection by the key returned by the given function.

```kotlin
fun <T, R : Comparable<R>> Iterable<T>.sortedBy(selector: (T) -> R?): List<T>
```
Returns a list of all elements sorted according to natural sort order of the value returned by the selector.

```kotlin
fun <T, K, V> Iterable<T>.associate(transform: (T) -> Pair<K, V>): Map<K, V>
```
Returns a Map containing key-value pairs provided by transform function applied to elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.first(): T
```
Returns the first element. Throws NoSuchElementException if the collection is empty.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.firstOrNull(): T?
```
Returns the first element, or null if the collection is empty.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.any(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if at least one element matches the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.all(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if all elements match the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.none(predicate: (T) -> Boolean): Boolean
```
Returns true if no elements match the given predicate.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.count(): Int
```
Returns the number of elements in the collection.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.joinToString(separator: CharSequence = ", "): String
```
Creates a string from all the elements separated using separator.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toList(): List<T>
```
Returns a List containing all elements.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toMutableList(): MutableList<T>
```
Returns a MutableList filled with all elements of this collection.

```kotlin
fun <T> Iterable<T>.toSet(): Set<T>
```
Returns a Set of all elements.

```kotlin
fun <K, V> Iterable<Pair<K, V>>.toMap(): Map<K, V>
```
Returns a new map containing all key-value pairs from the given collection of pairs.

```kotlin
fun String.toInt(): Int
```
Parses the string as a signed Int number and returns the result. Throws NumberFormatException if not valid.

```kotlin
fun String.toDouble(): Double
```
Parses the string as a Double number and returns the result. Throws NumberFormatException if not valid.

```kotlin
fun String.toIntOrNull(): Int?
```
Parses the string as a signed Int number and returns the result or null if the string is not a valid integer.

```kotlin
fun <T> runBlocking(block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T
```
Runs a new coroutine and blocks the current thread until its completion.

```kotlin
fun CoroutineScope.launch(block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit): Job
```
Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a Job.

```kotlin
fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): Deferred<T>
```
Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of Deferred.

```kotlin
suspend fun <T> withContext(context: CoroutineContext, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T
```
Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context and returns the result.

```kotlin
suspend fun delay(timeMillis: Long)
```
Delays coroutine for a given time without blocking a thread.

```kotlin
fun name(params): ReturnType { }
```
**Keyword** — Declares a function.

```kotlin
val name: Type = value
```
**Keyword** — Declares a read-only (immutable) property or local variable.

```kotlin
var name: Type = value
```
**Keyword** — Declares a mutable property or local variable.

```kotlin
class Name(params) { }
```
**Keyword** — Declares a class.

```kotlin
object Name { }
```
**Keyword** — Declares a singleton object or object expression.

```kotlin
interface Name { }
```
**Keyword** — Declares an interface that classes can implement.

```kotlin
when (value) {
    pattern -> result
    else -> default
}
```
**Keyword** — Pattern matching expression, similar to switch in other languages.

```kotlin
if (condition) { } else { }
```
**Keyword** — Conditional expression. Can also be used as an expression that returns a value.

```kotlin
for (item in collection) { }
```
**Keyword** — For loop that iterates through anything that provides an iterator.

```kotlin
while (condition) { }
```
**Keyword** — Loop that executes body while condition is true.
**Keyword** — Returns from the nearest enclosing function. Supports labeled returns with `return@label`.

```kotlin
throw Exception("message")
```
**Keyword** — Throws an exception. Throw expression has type Nothing.

```kotlin
try { } catch (e: Exception) { } finally { }
```
**Keyword** — Begins a try-catch exception handling block. Can also be used as an expression.
**Keyword** — Handles an exception type in a try block. Kotlin does not have checked exceptions.
**Keyword** — Block that always executes after try/catch, regardless of whether an exception was thrown.
**Keyword** — Marks a function or lambda as suspending (callable from within coroutines).

```kotlin
data class Name(val prop: Type)
```
**Keyword** — Marks a class as a data class. Auto-generates equals(), hashCode(), toString(), copy(), and componentN().

```kotlin
sealed class/interface Name { }
```
**Keyword** — Restricts class hierarchy. All direct subclasses must be in the same package.

```kotlin
enum class Name { VALUE1, VALUE2 }
```
**Keyword** — Declares an enum class with a fixed set of instances.
**Keyword** — Marks a member as overriding a superclass member or interface member. Required in Kotlin.
**Keyword** — Allows a class to be inherited from or a member to be overridden. Classes are final by default.
**Keyword** — Marks a class or member as abstract. Abstract members have no implementation and must be overridden.

```kotlin
companion object { }
```
**Keyword** — Declares a companion object inside a class, providing static-like members.
**Keyword** — Requests the compiler to inline the function and its lambda parameters at call sites.
**Keyword** — Allows calling a function with infix notation: `a func b` instead of `a.func(b)`.
**Keyword** — Marks a function as an operator overload (e.g., plus, get, invoke).

```kotlin
typealias Name = ExistingType
```
**Keyword** — Provides alternative names for existing types.

```kotlin
lateinit var name: Type
```
**Keyword** — Allows initializing a non-null var property outside of a constructor body.

```kotlin
const val NAME = value
```
**Keyword** — Marks a property as a compile-time constant. Must be top-level or companion object val of primitive/String.

```kotlin
class Int : Number, Comparable<Int>
```
Represents a 32-bit signed integer. Range: -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647.

```kotlin
class Long : Number, Comparable<Long>
```
Represents a 64-bit signed integer.

```kotlin
class Double : Number, Comparable<Double>
```
Represents a double-precision 64-bit IEEE 754 floating point number.

```kotlin
class Float : Number, Comparable<Float>
```
Represents a single-precision 32-bit IEEE 754 floating point number.

```kotlin
class Boolean : Comparable<Boolean>
```
Represents a value which is either `true` or `false`.

```kotlin
class String : Comparable<String>, CharSequence
```
Represents an array of characters. Strings are immutable in Kotlin.

```kotlin
class Char : Comparable<Char>
```
Represents a 16-bit Unicode character.

```kotlin
object Unit
```
The type with only one value: the Unit object. Corresponds to `void` in Java.

```kotlin
class Nothing
```
Nothing has no instances. Used as return type of functions that never return (e.g., throw, infinite loops).

```kotlin
open class Any
```
The root of the Kotlin class hierarchy. Every Kotlin class has `Any` as a superclass.

```kotlin
class Array<T>
```
Represents an array. Use `arrayOf()` to create an array.

```kotlin
interface List<out E> : Collection<E>
```
A generic ordered collection of elements. Read-only; use MutableList for modification.

```kotlin
interface MutableList<E> : List<E>, MutableCollection<E>
```
A generic ordered mutable collection supporting add and remove operations.

```kotlin
interface Map<K, out V>
```
A collection that holds pairs of objects (keys and values). Read-only.

```kotlin
interface MutableMap<K, V> : Map<K, V>
```
A modifiable collection that holds pairs of objects and supports adding/removing entries.

```kotlin
interface Set<out E> : Collection<E>
```
A generic unordered collection that does not support duplicate elements. Read-only.

```kotlin
interface MutableSet<E> : Set<E>, MutableCollection<E>
```
A modifiable collection of unique elements supporting add and remove operations.

```kotlin
data class Pair<out A, out B>(val first: A, val second: B)
```
Represents a generic pair of two values. Create with `to` infix function.

```kotlin
data class Triple<out A, out B, out C>(val first: A, val second: B, val third: C)
```
Represents a triad of three values.

```kotlin
interface Sequence<out T>
```
A sequence that returns values lazily through its iterator.

```kotlin
class Regex
```
Represents an immutable regular expression. Create with `Regex(pattern)` or `"pattern".toRegex()`.

```kotlin
interface Comparable<in T>
```
Classes that implement this interface have a defined total ordering between their instances.

```kotlin
interface Iterable<out T>
```
Classes that inherit from this interface can be represented as a sequence of elements that can be iterated over.

```kotlin
interface Collection<out E> : Iterable<E>
```
A generic collection of elements providing read-only methods.

```kotlin
data class Name(val prop1: Type1, val prop2: Type2, ...)
```
Declares a class whose primary purpose is holding data, automatically generating equals(), hashCode(), toString(), copy(), and componentN() destructuring functions from the primary constructor parameters. Data classes must have at least one val or var parameter and cannot be abstract, open, sealed, or inner.

```kotlin
interface Flow<out T>
```
An asynchronous data stream that sequentially emits values and completes normally or with an exception.

```kotlin
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```kotlin
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```kotlin
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```kotlin
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```kotlin
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
